docs.title: Saturn Engine
docs.text: fucked webiste

docs.title: Maths
docs.text: Saturn uses OpenGL Mathematics (GLM or glm) as it's internal maths library.

docs.title: Coordinate System
docs.text:
Saturn uses a left handed Cartesian coordinate system where Y+ points up. This means that on a 2D screen the origin is at the bottom left.

Because vulkan uses a right handed Cartesian coordinate system where Y+ points down and the orgin is at the top-left, this results in all of the objects submitted into the Renderer to be upside down. This is by design. As in the editor the image UV are flipped. However, in Dist the image is fipped via the TexturePass.

docs.title: Depth Buffer Values
docs.text: Saturn uses non-linear non-revsered depth, this means that an object close by will have a value of 0.xxxxx and objects far away will have ~1.0. Basically ~0 at the near plane and ~1 at the far plane.

docs.title: Use of Radians
docs.text:
Saturn internally uses radians for measurement for angles. However, it's recommened that you stay in degrees the whole way and convert your degrees into radians at the end of your calculation.

The Entity inspector in the SceneHierarchyPanel will display the rotational values as degrees.

docs.title: Camera Projection Matrix
docs.text:
The default near plane is 0.1f and the default far plane is 1000.0f.

The projection matrix is a Perspective FOV built with glm::perspectiveFovRH_ZO, which means right-handed coordinate system and depth 0 to 1.

docs.title: Alura
docs.text:
Alura uses a standard 2D screen-space coordinate system. Y+ goes down towards the bottom of the screen and X+ moves to the right.

Saturn will still flip the coordinates internally when it gets rendered, this is to avoid the UI being upside upon presentation.

docs.title: Renderer
docs.text:
This page gives the general overview of the Renderer as a system.

The renderer is structure like so:

Vulkan Context -> Renderer - > SceneRenderer

docs.title: SClass
docs.text:
An SClass defines the type of an SObject.

SCLASS()
class SClass : public SObject

docs.title: Functions (public)
docs.text:
// Construct the SObject that this SClass defines.
SObject* CreateDefaultObject();
SObject* CreateDefaultObject() const;

SClassFlags GetFlags() const;
const std::string& GetName() const;

size_t GetSize() const;
size_t GetAlignment() const;
uint64_t GetHash() const;

void SetFlag( SClassFlags flag );

const SClass* GetParentClass() const;
int GetPropertyCount() const;
const SProperty* const* GetProperties() const;

SProperty& GetProperty( const std::string& rPropertyName ) const;
bool IsChildOf( const SClass* pBase ) const;

docs.title: Static API
docs.text:
// Construct an SClass via it's SClassSpecification
static void RConstructClass();
static void RConstructClassHotReloaded();
static void ProcessNewlyLoadedSClasses();

std::filesystem::path HeaderPath;

docs.title: Related
docs.text: SClassSpecification

docs.title: SClassFlags
docs.text:
Flags for an SClass.

32 bit-field enum.

enum SClassFlags

docs.title: Valid flags
docs.text:
SC_None

// DO NOT SET! This flag is set by the reflection system itself!
SC_Initialised = 1 << 0,

// Is the class abstract
SC_Abstract   = 1 << 1,

// If a class is spawnable it means that it can be spawned into the scene.
SC_Spawnable  = 1 << 2,

// Visible in editor means that this class will appear when selecting a new class on the "Create a class" modal.
SC_VisibleInEditor = 1 << 3,

// By default classes have extended metadata, which means that classes will have their header path as path of the
// SClass, it's currently only used for game classes.
SC_NoExtendedMetadata = 1 << 4,

// Deprecated
SC_External = 1 << 5,

docs.title: Related
docs.text: SClass ClassMetadataHandler

docs.title: SClassSpecification
docs.text:
An SClassSpecification defines the specification of an SClass.

This struct is only to be used by the ClassMetadataHandler and the BuildTool!

struct SClassSpecification

docs.title: Members (public)
docs.text:
std::string Name;
std::underlying_type_t<SClassFlags> Flags;
int Properties;
size_t Size;
size_t Alignment;
uint64_t Hash;
SClass* pParentClass;
SObject* ( *pClassConstructor )();
SClass* ( *pStaticLinkFunction )();
const SProperty* const* SProperties;
SClassExtendedMetadata ClassExtendedMetadata;

docs.title: Related
docs.text: SClassExtendedMetadata SClass SClassFlags ClassMetadataHandler

docs.title: SObject
docs.text:
Base class for Gameplay Objects/Reflected types.

All classed based from SObject should have the S prefix.

class SObject : public RefTarget

docs.title: Functions (public)
docs.text:
const SClass* GetClass() const;
SObject* GetParentObject() const;

docs.title: Private members
docs.text:
// The class that we are an instance of.
SClass* m_pClass;

// The object that we reside in.
SObject* m_pParentObject;

docs.title: Version
docs.text:
Each version in Saturn has a display name and an internal number.

Starting in Saturn version 0.2.6, each version now comes with it's own build unqiue ID (UBID).

docs.title: Past Version Table
docs.text: Type Name Number Release Date Tag (UBID) Alpha 0.1.0 4096 27 March 2024 - Alpha 0.1.1 4097 27 May 2024 - Alpha 0.1.2 4098 27 July 2024 - Alpha 0.1.3 4099 27 Sept 2024 - Alpha 0.1.4 4100 1 Jan 2025 - Alpha 0.2.0 8192 27 March 2025 - Alpha 0.2.1 8193 27 May 2025 - Alpha 0.2.2 8194 27 July 2025 - Alpha 0.2.3 8195 1 Jan 2026 - Alpha 0.2.4 8196 27 March 2026 - Alpha 0.2.5 8197 27 May 2026 - Alpha 0.2.6 8198 27 July 2026 L27AP

docs.title: UBID
docs.text:
A UBID is randomly generated upon a final build before distributing the Engine.

A UBID can be 5 characters long or 6 characters long. The format must look like X99(9)XX e.g. A26AA or A260AA
